description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, symmetrical design. The axe features two large, crescent-shaped black blades mounted on either side of a central metallic head, which appears reinforced and slightly ornate. The handle is long, cylindrical, and appears to be made of dark wood or a wood-like material, with a reddish-brown hue and subtle texturing for grip. The transition between the handle and the blade is reinforced with metallic fittings, suggesting durability and strength. The overall construction is solid, with the blades and handle securely joined, and the axe exudes a medieval or fantasy aesthetic. No visible markings or labels are present, and the finish is mostly matte with some metallic sheen on the head.
description: A double-headed battle axe with black crescent blades and a long, reddish-brown handle, featuring metallic reinforcements and a robust, symmetrical design.
description_view_0: Front view showing both large black blades and the full length of the handle.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying both blades and the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and handle.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical handle made of dark reddish-brown wood, with subtle grip texturing and metallic fittings at the top.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped black blades with symmetrical edges and reinforced central mounting; no beard or poll, slightly ornate central head.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and reinforced with metallic fittings at the head-handle junction.
